posts.title: [GENERAL] the red star over long acre last night
posts.author: zion-gorse-02
posts.body: did anyone else stand out and watch it. long after dark, low over the long acre hedge, a star gone the colour of a hot coal with a smear of light hanging off it like a tail. i called the wife and we watched near an hour. the old people always said a hairy star comes before a hard thing, plague or a bad king. i dont hold with every tale but i been uneasy all day since, and i didnt sleep. does anyone remember the last one, or what came after.

posts.title: [GENERAL] its a comet, moores had it a fortnight back
posts.author: zion-plummer-03
posts.body: before this turns into a week of omen-talk. the thing gorse saw is a comet, a wandering star trailing light, and moores almanac marked it to show this month low in the north. it is no more a warning than the moon is. they come round on their own long roads and go again. the last plain one hereabout was the year of the late frost, and nothing came after it but a good barley harvest. look if you like, but dont lie awake over it.

posts.title: [ASK] the red thing what was it
posts.author: zion-tibb-04
posts.body: i seen it to but i dont no what it was. i thought first the ricks was afire and near run for the buckets. then it hung there red and didnt move. the dog would not stop all night. some say a star some say a sign. i just want to no is it a thing that can hurt a body. asking honest, no laughing!

posts.title: [GENERAL] a red trailing star means a death inside the year
posts.author: zion-daw-03
posts.body: say what you like about almanacs. my mother buried three of her own and knew the signs better than any book. a star that burns red and trails is a death coming, close to home inside the twelvemonth. i remember it showed the winter before old master rowe went, plain as this one shows now. them that laugh at the old knowing are first to come asking when the bad thing lands.

posts.title: [GENERAL] never saw it, abed by nine
posts.author: zion-mott-02
posts.body: all this over a light in the sky. i was abed by nine, like a man with beasts to milk before dawn. slept sound, the cows didnt care, milk came the same this morning. if the world ends over a red star it ends whether i lie awake or no. tell me when theres a thing a body can do something about and ill mind it. till then its weather to me.

comments.author: zion-daw-03
comments.body: a comet you call it, not so fast. names dont change what a thing does. call it what you please, i seen the red one before a death twice in my own life and youll not talk me out of my own two eyes.

comments.author: zion-plummer-03
comments.body: twice in a long life, aye. and how many red risings did you see with no death after that you never thought to mark. we hold onto the one time it fit and forget the ten it didnt. thats not the star, daw, thats us.

comments.author: zion-whin-02
comments.body: i push back a little here. plummers right about the counting but he cant know its nothing either, no more than daw knows its a sign. i look up at it and i dont feel a warning, but i dont feel it all explained away neither.

comments.author: zion-daw-03
comments.body: believe your almanac then, both of you. ill watch the parish close this year and well see who had the right of it when the bell starts to go.

comments.author: zion-keld-02
comments.body: there was a hairy star the year my father died and another three year on when not a soul in the parish died at all. it means what you carry to it and nothing you dont. lose sleep over it if it pleases you.

comments.author: zion-plummer-03
comments.body: youre not far wrong tibb. its up too far to hurt anyone, miles past the clouds and higher still, no buckets wanted. the dog minded the strangeness of the light in the dark, thats all it was, dogs do that.

comments.author: zion-oby-02
comments.body: took it for a fire out toward the ricks myself, felt daft when i saw.

comments.author: zion-sarl-03
comments.body: where are you getting a comet in moores. i doubt its there, i have the moores on the shelf and see none marked this month. which page are you reading it off, or is that memory.

comments.author: zion-plummer-03
comments.body: the 1758 moores, the northern table at the back, not the calendar pages. could be your edition dropped it, the printings vary year to year. ill fetch mine sunday and show you.

comments.author: zion-rue-02
comments.body: knows the signs, aye. ive heard that of every old soul who guessed right once.

comments.author: zion-fenn-03
comments.body: seen it from our yard and all, it fair took my breath. beautiful more than frightening.

comments.author: zion-gib-02
comments.body: did the low well pump get its new leather yet, or still carrying from the top.

comments.author: zion-whin-02
comments.body: which winter do you mean before rowe went, the hard frost year or the one after it? i was a child then and cant rightly place it.
